claims.summary: The fixed LCG constants and projection kernel determine the complete legacy 768D-to-8D basis, so changing them would break compatibility.
claims.disposition: Verified the fixed-kernel checksum, compatibility-pinned basis policy, projection epoch identity, and deterministic source implementation.

claims.summary: A near-zero projection column could silently collapse one semantic texture dimension.
claims.disposition: Verified projection_basis_health_v1 and its regression: all column norms are finite and near unit, no dead dimension is present, and the weakest raw column remains more than 10,000 times above the review threshold.

claims.summary: OnceLock initialization and accumulation precision should repeat deterministically.
claims.disposition: Verified the repeated-bit-exact fixed and dynamic probes plus the f64 reference-delta audit; no live accumulator migration is inferred.

claims.summary: The eight projected outputs should remain distinct owners of semantic dimensions 32 through 39 rather than aliasing texture meanings.
claims.disposition: Verified the explicit eight-slot mapping and distinct semantic texture labels in source and codec structure tests; this proves ownership, not felt distinctness.

claims.summary: Changing the LCG constants, basis threshold response, or live projection matrix would alter codec transport.
claims.disposition: Preserve an exact Tier 5 Mike/operator wait with captured replay, compatibility evidence, and a new basis epoch required before any live projection change.
